name: Eekso — The Scholar's Archive
dmNotes: THE LORE ROOM (Claude prep 2026-07-16). This is the delivery system for the network rules — the slave-scholar's surviving research, plus instruments that can read Felson's bottle. Reveals are TIERED in the script callouts: Tier 1 is safe (locked canon, cheap to give), Tier 2 costs a test/risk, Tier 3 (⚑) is yours to grant or hold. The ghostly bed is cued for the moment the bottle answers — don't autoplay it.
scriptText:
# The Scholar's Archive

The door is dwarven, but the lock is elvish — delicate work, picked from the inside more than once. Beyond it: a long vaulted room the water never reached. Shelves. Instruments of copper and crystal, green with age. And paper — years of it, in a hand that starts patient and neat and does not end that way. {{sfx:book-open}}

Every page is headed the same way: a catalogue number, a date in a calendar you don't know — and the imprint of a **collar seal**, pressed into wax, the way an owner brands a tool.

## The Research

{{music:cost}} {{sfx:pages}} They were made to study the thing in the water. They kept studying it long after whoever held their collar stopped collecting the reports.

> [!dm] **Tier 1 (free, locked canon):** the couriers carry harvested blueprints/minds/souls BACK to the pool · still water means a LARGE pool · the network shares what one node learns. The last pages just say **IT CONSUMES**, over and over.

> [!dm] **Tier 2 (costs a test on the bottle):** see below. **Tier 3 (⚑ your call):** anything pointing at the CULT pool, Eilistraee, or what 'true escape' takes — hold it unless the table earns it.

## The Bottle

The scholar's instruments still work. A crystal cradle, sized for a sample jar — sized, in fact, almost exactly for the potion bottle in Felsen's pack.

{{sfx:test}} The liquid climbs the glass before the test even begins — *reaching* for the instrument. {{amb:ghostly-loop|vol=30%|in=5s}} {{sfx:reacts}}

> [!dm] **Tier 2 reveals per test (pick what they earn):** the fragments are PIECES OF PEOPLE — copied, not stolen · the courier was returning to report; its cargo never arrived, and the network will notice the gap · the bottle answers when the artery is near (compass to pools/couriers — a tool AND a beacon). Kill the ghostly bed ({{amb:ghostly-loop|out=6s}}) when they stopper it.

ideas.text: The bottle as a compass that points both ways — it finds the pool, the network can find IT
The final ledger entry is unfinished mid-sentence — the collar outside the shed upstream finishes the story
Varen's divination + the instruments: one question answered honestly (seed for the release-mechanism thread)

entities.name: The scholar's ledgers
entities.note: Years of pool research. Portable, heavy, priceless.

entities.name: Crystal testing cradle
entities.note: Reads courier fragments. Too delicate to move?

entities.name: The slave-scholar (absent)
entities.note: Collared Thundrak Dûrn property. Taken — thread to the city.
